transcript: I saw the flickering shadows of love on your blind you were my woman as you deceived me I watched and went out of my mind my Delilah why Delilah. Why, Delilah? I could see that you were no good for me, but I was lost, like a slave that no man could free. At the break of day, when that man drove away, I was waiting. Across the street to your house, when you opened the door, you stood there, laughing. I felt the knife in my hand and you laughed no more. My Delilah. Why, Delilah? So, before they come to break down the door, forgive me, Delilah. I just couldn't take it anymore.
